---
applyTo: "**/*.{md,yml,yaml,json}"
---

# Writing Instructions

## Goal

Write natural, precise American English that preserves technical meaning and can be understood on the first reading by humans and agents.

## Rules

- Preserve facts, evidence status, scope, authority, safety boundaries, user vocabulary, identifiers, commands, URLs, quotations, and established terms before improving style.
- Name the actor and action when responsibility, permission, ownership, or verification matters.
- Give each sentence one main relationship. Split a sentence when independent actions, evidence, or qualifications compete for attention.
- Rewrite dense stacks of labels or modifiers. Use conventional compounds and hyphens when they prevent misreading, but do not coin a new compound label when an ordinary verb phrase is clearer.
- Use a contrast only when both sides are relevant and genuinely differ. State the selected claim directly when the rejected alternative adds no meaning.
- Remove canned transitions, repeated sentence frames, redundant conclusions, decorative summaries, and claims that lack a subject, scope, or evidence.
- Use the smallest structure that fits the content. Use a list for a real set, sequence, or comparison, not to make short prose appear comprehensive.
- Prefer concrete verbs and familiar words, but retain and define technical language that carries necessary meaning.
- Revise subtractively first. Add words only to restore a missing actor, definition, relationship, evidence limit, exception, or safety boundary.
- Do not infer authorship from a writing pattern or edit prose merely to make it appear less generated.

## Review

Before editing, record the meaning that must survive. After editing, compare the revision with that meaning and confirm every protected literal remains exact.

Use [Writing Quality](../../docs/WRITING.md) when a prose rule changes, a review is disputed, or a change needs the source hierarchy, diagnostics, exceptions, regression method, or maintenance budget.
